definition: Apnea-Hypopnea Index. The average number of breathing events (apnoeas + hypopnoeas) per hour. Many clinicians aim for under 5 on therapy, but targets are individual.
Obstructive Apnoea. A breathing pause caused by the airway physically collapsing or being blocked.
Central Apnoea. A breathing pause where the brain briefly fails to send the signal to breathe. Different mechanism to obstructive apnoea.
Hypopnoea. A partial reduction in airflow rather than a full pause. Counted alongside apnoeas in the AHI.
Respiratory Effort Related Arousal. A brief awakening triggered by increased effort to breathe, even when airflow doesn't drop enough to count as a hypopnoea.
A subtle narrowing of the airway that hasn't yet become a full hypopnoea or apnoea. CPAP/APAP machines often respond to it preventively.
How much air is escaping around the mask, usually measured in litres per minute. High leak can reduce therapy effectiveness and cause dry mouth.
The pressure at or below which the machine spent 95% of the night. Useful for understanding what your APAP machine is settling at.
A regulatory term, often defined as using CPAP at least 4 hours a night on at least 70% of nights over 30 days. Your clinic may use a different definition.
Adding moisture to CPAP airflow via a heated water chamber. Helps with dry mouth and nasal symptoms.
